senses.definition: the act of fitting again or renovating equipment or a vehicle
examples: The ship underwent a quick refitment before the voyage.
They scheduled refitment of the fleet to improve armor.

senses.definition: plural of refitment; multiple acts of fitting again or renovating equipment or a vehicle
examples: The shipyards announced several refitments for the fleet.
Gaps in maintenance were addressed during the refitments.

senses.definition: the act or process of fitting something again or altering it for a different use
examples: The submarine returns to port for a refit.
They did a quick refit of the engine.

senses.definition: to fit or equip again; to modify for a different purpose
examples: They refit the car with a new sound system.
The team refits the workspace to improve collaboration.

senses.definition: past tense of refit: fitted again or modified for a different use
examples: The vessel was refitted last year.
She refitted the apartment to include a home office.

senses.definition: the act of fitting again or modifying equipment or a space
examples: The refitting of the engine took several days.
A refitting project is planned for the ship's hull.

senses.definition: plural of refitting; multiple acts of fitting again
examples: Several refittings were completed during the overhaul.
We reviewed the refittings scheduled for the fleet.

senses.definition: to fix again or to apply a prefix; in some contexts, to shorten to 'prefix' as a root form error
examples: If the label is wrong, you can refix it.
They decided to refix the wiring diagram.

senses.definition: a prefix or the act of placing a prefix; less common usage
examples: The word's refix changes its meaning.
In linguistics, a refix modifies root meaning.

senses.definition: the act of attaching or forming a new prefix to a word
examples: Refixation is common in language evolution.
They studied the refixation patterns in German.

senses.definition: past tense of refix: attached a prefix to; fixed again
examples: The word was refixed to convey a different meaning.
They refixed the document with the correct heading.

senses.definition: plural of prefix; the set of prefixes attached to a word
examples: Prefixes and refixes influence word meaning.
Common refixes include un-, re-, and pre-.

senses.definition: third person singular of refix; attaches a prefix to a word
examples: The editor prefixes the base term with un-.
She refixes common roots for clarity.

senses.definition: the action of adding a prefix to a word or altering a word by prefixes
examples: Refixing is common in agglutinative languages.
They described the process of refixing the term.

senses.definition: a thing that is refixed; a reassembled or reconfigured fixture
examples: The refixture of the stage improved sightlines.
A new refixture of the lab benches was completed.

senses.definition: abbreviation or shorthand; may refer to reflectance or a registered term in branding, context dependent
examples: The spec mentions refl for reflectivity measurements.
In the sheet, refl stands for reflector value.

senses.definition: to flag or mark again; to raise a new flag or to indicate a change in status
examples: They decided to reflag the project as high priority.
The port authorities reflag the ship after inspections.

senses.definition: the act of flagging again; a renewed flag signal
examples: There was a reflag of the vessel to indicate danger.
The reflag prompted new safety procedures.

senses.definition: to whip or lash repeatedly; a rare or archaic term often used in moral or religious contexts
examples: The historical text describes rebels to be reflagellated as punishment.
In old chronicles, captives were reflagellated for discipline.

senses.definition: past tense of reflag; marked again with a flag or signal indicating status change
examples: The ship was reflagged to indicate its new destination.
The territory was reflagged after negotiations.

senses.definition: the act of flagging again to signal status change
examples: Reflagging of the ship occurred after the treaty.
There was extensive reflagging within the border controls.

senses.definition: plural of reflag; multiple instances of flagging again
examples: The reflags around the harbor indicate restricted access.
Security checks included several reflags.

senses.definition: third person singular of reflag; marks again with a flag or status
examples: The agency reflags the product for export control.
They reflags the route as unsafe.

senses.definition: to add flair or attractiveness again; to enhance appearance or presentation
examples: They tried to reflair the booth with brighter signage.
A new color scheme was used to reflair the product range.

senses.definition: to flame again or rekindle; to resume bright or intense appearance
examples: The stage lights reflame as the curtain rises.
The city reflames with energy after the festival.

senses.definition: to refresh or update (data or a display) by loading again
examples: Please reflash the page to see the latest updates.
The device will reflash its firmware automatically.

senses.definition: to inflate again; to raise inflation or level of prices (rare)
examples: Economic measures attempted to reflate demand.
The policy aimed to reflate the economy.

senses.definition: past tense of reflate; inflated again or stimulated economy previously
examples: The market reflated after the stimulus package.
Prices reflated during the recovery.

senses.definition: third person singular of reflate; inflates again or stimulates again
examples: The policy reflates the economy.
New measures reflates consumer demand.

senses.definition: the act of inflating again or stimulating again
examples: Reflating policies took time to show effects.
They discussed reflating strategies in the meeting.
